title: [Bug]: open-release-pr should avoid gh pr edit when refreshing existing PR bodies
summary: The local pull request helper still refreshes existing PR title and body text through gh pr edit, which can fail with an unrelated GitHub GraphQL projectCards deprecation warning.
observed: A live PR refresh continued after gh pr edit failed, but the pull request body was not updated through the intended command path.
expected: Existing pull request title and body refresh should use the GitHub issue edit path against the PR number, matching the safer label synchronization path.
reproduction: Run scripts/open-release-pr.sh for an existing pull request in a repository where gh pr edit title/body refresh hits the GraphQL projectCards deprecation failure.
failing_test: Add a shell-helper regression test proving existing PR refresh uses gh issue edit rather than gh pr edit.
impact: PR metadata can become stale even though the helper continues. Runtime package behavior, NATS delivery, sink writes, DLQ handling, and ACK ordering are unaffected.
delivery_semantics: This bug is limited to GitHub workflow metadata automation and cannot affect commit-then-ACK behavior.
security: The fix must keep PR body content sanitized and must not introduce secrets, private endpoints, payloads, or token values into generated PR text.
acceptance: Existing pull request refresh uses gh issue edit against the PR number.
The helper no longer depends on gh pr edit for title and body refresh.
A deterministic fake-gh shell regression covers the existing PR path.
The PR helper still refuses to run from main.
tests: Run python -m pytest tests/unit/test_pr_auto_approval.py tests/unit/test_pr_label_sync.py -q and scripts/check.sh.
documentation: No new public documentation is required beyond keeping workflow notes and release notes accurate.
closeout: Close after the bug branch merges into the release branch and release-gated closure confirms the release has shipped.
